 {"id":"bd-xs2m","title":"Conformance: UI Editor Extensions (rainbow-editor, modal-editor, message-renderer)","description":"Conformance tests for custom editor/renderer extensions. Extensions: 1. rainbow-editor.ts — Animated rainbow content in editor 2. modal-editor.ts — Custom modal editor overlay 3. message-renderer.ts — Custom message rendering pipeline. Tests: registration, editor creation, content rendering with UI mocks, cleanup on close.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:18:21.809469535Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:38:14.530671054Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T01:38:14.530523128Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xs2m","depends_on_id":"bd-3p5w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:18:21.819818438Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xs79","title":"Extension load-time benchmarks (all must load <100ms)","description":"Create benchmarks that measure extension load time for every extension in the conformance suite. Requirements: 1. Measure time from QuickJS context creation to register() callback completion 2. Every extension must load in <100ms (P99) 3. Measure both cold start (fresh context) and warm start (cached bytecode) 4. Output: JSON report with per-extension load times, P50/P95/P99 stats 5. Fail the benchmark if any extension exceeds 100ms. Run 100 iterations per extension for statistical significance. Separate by tier (simple vs complex vs community) for analysis.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:19:48.314941502Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:53:31.957077430Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:53:31.956991099Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xs79","depends_on_id":"bd-3p98","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:19:48.706426241Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":629,"issue_id":"bd-xs79","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented per-extension P99 load-time budget checks in ext_bench_harness.rs (bd-20s9 harness). Changes: (1) Added ext_cold_load_per_ext_p99 budget check (100ms threshold) with worst-offender tracking, (2) Added ext_warm_load_per_ext_p99 budget check (100ms threshold), (3) Added worst_extension field to BudgetCheck struct for identifying slow extensions, (4) Added per-extension load times table to markdown report showing Cold/Warm P50/P95/P99 per extension, (5) Increased nightly iterations from 50 to 100 for statistical significance. All 5 budget checks pass in debug mode except cold P99 for one community extension (expected — debug is 2-3x slower). All tests pass (21 scenarios, 44 total).","created_at":"2026-02-07T00:53:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xy0qq","title":"FUZZ-DOC: fuzz/README.md — Document harness usage, crash triage workflow, seed corpus management","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:16:53.746803979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T22:21:38.324339026Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T22:21:38.324315683Z","close_reason":"Added fuzz/README.md runbook for harness usage, crash triage, and corpus management","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["documentation","fuzz"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xy0qq","depends_on_id":"bd-291y7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:17:21.860720948Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1497,"issue_id":"bd-xy0qq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## FUZZ-DOC: fuzz/README.md Documentation

### Purpose
Comprehensive documentation for the fuzzing infrastructure so any developer or agent can:
1. Understand why fuzzing exists and what it covers
2. Run fuzz targets locally
3. Interpret results
4. Triage crashes
5. Add new fuzz targets
6. Understand the CI integration

### Document Structure

```markdown
# Fuzzing Infrastructure

## Overview
- Why we fuzz (parser-heavy system, untrusted data sources)
- What's covered (SSE, JSONL, providers, messages, config, tools, extensions)
- Two fuzzing layers: proptest (in-process) + libfuzzer (coverage-guided)

## Quick Start
\`\`\`bash
# Run all proptests
cargo test -- proptest

# Run a specific fuzz target
cargo fuzz run fuzz_sse_parser -- -max_total_time=60

# Run the full E2E validation
./scripts/fuzz_e2e.sh --quick
\`\`\`

## Fuzz Targets
| Target | File | What it fuzzes | Priority |
|--------|------|---------------|----------|
| fuzz_sse_parser | fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_sse_parser.rs | SSE feed_into/flush | P0 |
| fuzz_provider_event | ... | Provider process_event() | P0 |
| ... | ... | ... | ... |

## Adding a New Fuzz Target
1. Create fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_<name>.rs
2. Add seed corpus in fuzz/corpus/fuzz_<name>/
3. Verify: cargo fuzz run fuzz_<name> -- -max_total_time=60
4. Add to the CI matrix in .github/workflows/fuzz.yml
5. Update this README

## Crash Triage Workflow
1. Crash found: fuzz/artifacts/<target>/crash-<hash>
2. Minimize: cargo fuzz tmin <target> fuzz/artifacts/<target>/crash-<hash>
3. Categorize: OOM, panic, timeout, logic error
4. Fix the root cause in source code
5. Move minimized input to fuzz/regression/<target>/
6. Create regression test (see tests/fuzz_regression.rs)
7. Re-run: cargo fuzz run <target> -- -max_total_time=300

## Seed Corpus Management
- Seeds are committed to git in fuzz/corpus/
- Extracted from VCR cassettes and test fixtures
- Edge case seeds manually created for known risks
- Corpus grows during fuzzing (new inputs auto-added)

## CI Integration
- Quick runs (60s) on every push to main
- Nightly deep runs (30min) for P0 targets
- Corpus cached across CI runs via GitHub Actions cache
- Crashes uploaded as artifacts

## Architecture
\`\`\`
fuzz/
├── Cargo.toml          # libfuzzer-sys + arbitrary + pi_agent_rust[fuzzing]
├── fuzz_targets/       # One .rs file per harness
├── corpus/             # Seed inputs (committed)
├── artifacts/          # Crash inputs (gitignored)
├── crashes/            # Minimized crashes (committed, categorized)
├── regression/         # Fixed crash inputs (committed, regression-tested)
└── reports/            # Validation reports (gitignored)
\`\`\`

## Feature Flag: fuzzing
The main crate exposes internal APIs under a \`fuzzing\` feature flag.
The fuzz crate enables this: \`pi_agent_rust = { path = "..", features = ["fuzzing"] }\`
This keeps #![forbid(unsafe_code)] intact in the main crate.
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- fuzz/README.md exists with all sections above
- All fuzz targets are documented with their purpose and priority
- Crash triage workflow is step-by-step with example commands
- Adding a new target has a clear checklist
- Document is self-contained (no external links required for understanding)","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:19:55Z"},{"id":1618,"issue_id":"bd-xy0qq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed `bd-xy0qq` by adding `fuzz/README.md` with:

- current harness inventory (all registered targets in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`)
- required execution pattern using `rch exec -- cargo fuzz ...`
- tmpfs guidance (`CARGO_TARGET_DIR`/`TMPDIR`) for heavy runs
- seed corpus management guidance and source fixture references
- crash triage workflow (repro, minimization, regression follow-up)
- multi-agent coordination notes (file reservations + thread updates)

This documentation is intended as the operator runbook for ongoing fuzzing work.
","created_at":"2026-02-14T22:21:37Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-y20iz","title":"DROPIN-175: Build end-to-end parity script suite across all execution surfaces with rich logging","description":"Create comprehensive E2E scripts for interactive, print, JSON mode, RPC, and SDK pathways, capturing step-level logs, artifacts, and diff-friendly transcripts.","design":"Create full-surface E2E scripts covering interactive, print, JSON mode, RPC, and SDK workflows with step-by-step logging, environment capture, transcript artifacts, and differential summaries.","acceptance_criteria":"E2E suite runs reproducibly, emits detailed per-step logs and artifacts, and validates end-user and integrator workflows end-to-end.","notes":"This is the closest approximation to real-world drop-in usage.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SwiftValley","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:50:49.346671226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T02:34:06.704742763Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dropin","e2e","logging","parity","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-y20iz","depends_on_id":"bd-2m6qw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:50:58.827538917Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-y20iz","depends_on_id":"bd-2xalc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:50:59.571267931Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-y20iz","depends_on_id":"bd-31hfi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:50:59.074553320Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-y20iz","depends_on_id":"bd-3ig1e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:50:59.324830713Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1561,"issue_id":"bd-y20iz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Context: parity confidence must include real end-to-end workflows across every execution surface. This task ensures complete scenario-level coverage with forensic-grade logging.","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:50:52Z"},{"id":1671,"issue_id":"bd-y20iz","author":"SwiftValley","text":"Implemented first bd-y20iz slice: added RPC execution surface to golden-corpus E2E parity suite. Changes: (1) tests/e2e_golden_corpus.rs now runs golden_corpus_rpc_mode and includes rpc_mode in required manifest coverage; (2) added fixtures tests/golden_corpus/rpc_mode/rpc_get_state_smoke.json and tests/golden_corpus/rpc_mode/rpc_unknown_command_error.json for success+failure RPC command paths. Verification via rch: cargo test --test e2e_golden_corpus golden_corpus_rpc_mode -- --exact (pass), cargo test --test e2e_golden_corpus golden_corpus_manifest_coverage -- --exact (pass), full cargo test --test e2e_golden_corpus (106 passed, 1 ignored).","created_at":"2026-02-15T02:37:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-y2qy","title":"Docs: providers.md (env vars + supported providers)","description":"# Goal
Create `docs/providers.md` documenting supported providers and how to configure API keys.

# Source Material
- Legacy: `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/providers.md`
- Rust: `src/auth.rs`, `src/providers/*`

# Must Include
- Supported providers and their env vars.
- Notes on OAuth vs API keys (what is supported in Rust currently).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] If behavior changes, add unit tests for success/failure + edge cases; otherwise note N/A explicitly in notes
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:49:32.172039026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:30:47.602465778Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T08:10:27.465698417Z","close_reason":"Completed: docs/providers.md covers providers, env vars, and OAuth note","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-y2qy","depends_on_id":"bd-3m7f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-yd9","title":"Define extension fixture schema + normalization contract","description":"Background:
- Extension conformance requires a fixture schema that captures inputs and expected outputs precisely.

Steps:
- Define JSON schema for extension fixtures (inputs, outputs, metadata, normalization hints).
- Specify normalization rules (paths/time/ANSI/UUID) and how hints are applied.
- Provide example fixtures for tools, slash commands, and event hooks.
- Document how to add new cases and validate fixtures.

Acceptance:
- Schema can represent tools, slash commands, and event hooks uniformly.
- Fixture validation is deterministic and ready for unit tests.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:24:33.255817422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:26:36.282102927Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:29:56.253765719Z","close_reason":"Defined extension fixture schema + normalization contract in CONFORMANCE.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
